title: Modular Code — Functions
description: Refactor your program by extracting logic into at least three functions: calculateAverage() that takes three grades and returns the average, getLetterGrade() that takes an average and returns a letter grade, and displayStudent() that prints the student info. Call these functions from main.
test_code:
cpp_content = cpp
# Check for at least 3 function definitions (return_type name(params) {)
import re
func_pattern = r'(double|char|void|int|string)\s+\w+\s*\([^)]*\)\s*\{'
functions_found = re.findall(func_pattern, cpp_content)
# Subtract 1 for main
non_main_funcs = [f for f in re.finditer(r'(double|char|void|int|string)\s+(\w+)\s*\([^)]*\)\s*\{', cpp_content) if f.group(2) != 'main']
assert len(non_main_funcs) >= 3, f'Must define at least 3 functions besides main, found {len(non_main_funcs)}'
assert 'calculateAverage' in cpp or 'calculate_average' in cpp or 'calcAverage' in cpp, 'Must have a function for calculating the average'
assert 'getLetterGrade' in cpp or 'get_letter_grade' in cpp or 'letterGrade' in cpp, 'Must have a function for getting the letter grade'
assert 'displayStudent' in cpp or 'display_student' in cpp or 'printStudent' in cpp, 'Must have a function for displaying student info'
acceptance_criteria: Defines calculateAverage() that takes 3 grades and returns the average
Defines getLetterGrade() that takes an average and returns a char letter grade
Defines displayStudent() that prints student name, average, and letter grade
All three functions are called from main
hints: Function signature: double calculateAverage(double g1, double g2, double g3) { return (g1+g2+g3)/3.0; }
getLetterGrade takes a double and returns a char: char getLetterGrade(double avg) { ... }
displayStudent can return void: void displayStudent(string name, double avg, char grade) { ... }
Define functions above main — C++ requires functions to be declared before they are called
